original_abstract: Statistical heterogeneity remains a fundamental challenge in federated learning (FL), often degrading the generalization performance of global models across diverse client populations. To mitigate this, we introduce Federated Learning with Adaptive Local Aggregation (FedALA), a novel framework designed to intelligently extract relevant information from the global model to enhance personalized local models. At the core of our approach is the Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module. Before each local training phase, the ALA module adaptively merges the downloaded global parameters with the existing local state, tailoring the initialization to better align with the specific objectives of each client. We conducted comprehensive experiments across five benchmark datasets in both computer vision and natural language processing. The results demonstrate that FedALA consistently surpasses eleven state-of-the-art baselines, improving test accuracy by up to 3.27%. Additionally, we show that the ALA module is highly versatile; when integrated into other popular FL frameworks, it yields substantial performance gains, increasing test accuracy by as much as 24.19%. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/TsingZ0/FedALA.
humanized_abstract: Statistical heterogeneity remains one of the most prominent challenges in federated learning (FL), and models learned by FL often suffer from poor generalization performance on individual clients’ datasets due to this heterogeneity. In this work, we propose a new FL framework, called Federated Learning with Adaptive Local Aggregation (FedALA), that learns how to distill useful information from a global model for improving the local models. To this end, we introduce a new Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module, which is highly effective in boosting the performance of the local models. Prior to each local training round, the ALA module combines the global model parameters shared by the server at the beginning of the round with the local model’s parameters from the previous round to obtain an appropriate set of initialization parameters for the current round on each client. Our experimental results on five widely used datasets (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, STL-10, YFCC16 and ATIS) in both vision and language domains show that the proposed FedALA approach significantly outperforms 11 state-of-the-art baseline methods with an average of up to 3.27% improved accuracy. The ALA module can also be plugged into existing FL frameworks to gain performance. Our experiment shows that, when the ALA module is plugged into local updates and local warehousing, the ALA module alone can improve the top-1 accuracy by up to 24.19%. The implementation of FedALA is available online in the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/TsingZ0/FedALA.

document.output: Statistical heterogeneity remains one of the most prominent challenges in federated learning (FL), and models learned by FL often suffer from poor generalization performance on individual clients’ datasets due to this heterogeneity. In this work, we propose a new FL framework, called Federated Learning with Adaptive Local Aggregation (FedALA), that learns how to distill useful information from a global model for improving the local models. To this end, we introduce a new Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module, which is highly effective in boosting the performance of the local models. Prior to each local training round, the ALA module combines the global model parameters shared by the server at the beginning of the round with the local model’s parameters from the previous round to obtain an appropriate set of initialization parameters for the current round on each client. Our experimental results on five widely used datasets (CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, STL-10, YFCC16 and ATIS) in both vision and language domains show that the proposed FedALA approach significantly outperforms 11 state-of-the-art baseline methods with an average of up to 3.27% improved accuracy. The ALA module can also be plugged into existing FL frameworks to gain performance. Our experiment shows that, when the ALA module is plugged into local updates and local warehousing, the ALA module alone can improve the top-1 accuracy by up to 24.19%. The implementation of FedALA is available online in the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/TsingZ0/FedALA.
